commit.message:
Clear placeholder text when reconnecting to running scan

When a user selects a repo and auto-reconnects to a running experiment,
or clicks 'Watch Running Scan' from the modal, the initial placeholder
text ('Scan output will appear here...') was still visible.

Now the placeholder is cleared when reconnecting, so users see:
- Only the reconnection and status messages
- Any past output that was preserved in the DOM
- Real-time updates as polling continues

This prevents the confusing UX where both the placeholder message
and the actual scan output appear together.

Fix: Ensure scan processes survive browser disconnect

Add 'start_new_session=True' to subprocess.Popen() call. This creates
the scan process in a new process group, ensuring it continues running
even if:
- The HTTP request is closed
- The user navigates away from the page
- The user refreshes the page
- The Flask server receives SIGTERM

Without this, the process would be terminated when the parent Flask
worker thread exits, killing the scan even though it was designed to
be server-persistent.

This is critical for background scans that should complete regardless
of browser connection status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prevent duplicate polling loops when reconnecting to running scan

Track polling interval in global variable currentPollInterval so we can
cancel any existing polling before starting a new one. This fixes the issue
where reconnecting multiple times would create multiple polling loops,
causing duplicate log messages.

Changes:
- Add currentPollInterval global variable
- Clear existing polling before starting new polling loop
- Set currentPollInterval to null when polling completes or times out
- Clear polling when user clicks reset button

This ensures only one polling loop runs at a time, preventing:
- Duplicate reconnect messages
- Duplicate progress updates
- Out-of-order elapsed time messages
- Resource waste from multiple simultaneous polling loops

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(keepalive): hold strong reference to ping task to prevent GC

asyncio.create_task returns a Task that the event loop holds only via a
weak reference. Without a strong reference the GC can silently discard
the task before it ever pings, making the keepalive a no-op.

Store the task in a module-level set and discard it via done_callback so
the reference is released cleanly when (if) the loop ever exits.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix build error: use CSS background-image instead of for hero sections Vite resolves paths as module imports at build time, causing Rollup to fail when the image files don't yet exist in public/images/. Using inline style background-image url() is not processed by Rollup, so the build succeeds and images load at runtime when added by the client. * docs(benchmarks): replace interpolation comparison with power-of-two fair test

Replace the SRHT vs exact comparison table in README with results from
the new methodology: run both modes at d in {128,256,512,1024} where
next_power_of_two(d)=d, so both modes produce exactly n=d codes with
identical bit budgets and codebooks.

Key findings:
- d<=256: differences within noise (+-0.012 R@1), no consistent winner
- d=512: exact consistently +0.2-0.9 pp R@1
- d=1024 b=4: exact +3.8 pp R@1 — meaningful advantage for Haar-random QR
- d=1024 b=8: both modes at 79.6% R@1, indistinguishable near ceiling
- Speed: SRHT 1.1-17.9x faster; gap widens with d (O(d log d) vs O(d^2))

Also rewrites compare_quantizers_fair.py to use this methodology with
enforce-via-assert_power_of_two(), direct delta table (no interpolation),
and 100k synthetic Gaussian corpus at all dims.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(engine): v0.5 sprint — #29 #30 #31 #33 #34 + fast_mode dequantize fix

### #33 WAL write coalescing
Pre-build entire batch into one Vec<u8>, then one write_all + one flush
per append_batch call. Eliminates per-entry syscall overhead.

### #29 SIMD unpack_mse_indices
AVX2 variant for b=4: 16 packed bytes → 32 u16 per iteration via
_mm_unpacklo/hi_epi8 + _mm256_cvtepu8_epi16. Scalar fallback unchanged.

### #34 Cross-query parallelism in search_batch
For ANN or small-N (≤SEQ_THRESHOLD=20k) paths, par_iter() across queries.
Large-N brute-force stays serial (inner par_chunks saturates Rayon there).

### #31 Range index for metadata
BTreeMap<u64, Vec<u32>> per numeric field using IEEE 754 ordered-key
encoding (f64_to_ord). extract_range_condition() routes $gt/$gte/$lt/$lte
filters through get_range_candidates() in resolve_filter_via_index().

### #30 Auto query planner
_use_ann: Option<bool> (None=auto, True=force ANN, False=force brute).
auto_use_ann() returns true when index exists + N≥10k + delta≤20% of N.
Module-level constants: AUTO_ANN_THRESHOLD=10_000, AUTO_ANN_MAX_DELTA=20%.

### fix(quantizer): fast_mode dequantize
dequantize() short-circuits to MSE-only when fast_mode=True, preventing
a panic on the zero-length QJL slice during rerank.

* fix(python): change defaults to rerank=False, fast_mode=True (minimum disk out-of-box)

Previous defaults (rerank=True, fast_mode=False) stored INT8 raw vectors and QJL
residuals for every new database, making the zero-config experience heavier than
needed. New defaults match the paper Figure 5 allocation and minimize disk usage:

  rerank=False  — no raw vector storage; pure compressed codes
  fast_mode=True — MSE-only quantization; fastest ingest, minimum disk
  quantizer_type=None ("dense") — unchanged

Users who need higher recall explicitly opt in:
  rerank=True           → +5–25 pp R@1, ~2–4× more disk
  fast_mode=False       → +5–10 pp R@1 at d≥1536 when rerank=False

Update PYTHON_API.md and CONFIGURATION.md to reflect new defaults throughout:
parameter table, fast_mode×rerank combination table, all preset examples,
and the decision flowchart.

fix: always append signal protocol instructions to every prompt

Signal instructions (status.json updates, inbox checks, headless mode)
were only in the generic fallback. Custom prompts via --prompt,
--prompt-file, or .squad/launch-prompt.md templates bypassed them,
so headless teams never updated status.json.

Now signalInstructions() is appended to ALL resolved prompts regardless
of source. The 4-tier prompt resolution still works the same way for
the mission/task content — signal protocol is always added on top.

chore(tooling): Add prepare/restore modes for publish-readmes script

The readmes publish process previously accepted a single 'root' or 'sdk'
mode that could overwrite local README files and lacked a safe restore path.

The script now supports verb:target syntax (prepare:extension,
prepare:sdk, restore:extension, restore:sdk). It creates backups before
modifying README.md and restores them after publishing.

This preserves local README state and makes per-package publishing safer. The
targeted approach was chosen over a single 'root' mode because it avoids
accidental loss of local changes.

Add clipboard builtins for graphics programs:

gfx builtin pattern, plus regenerated README and copilot instructions.

Revert overly-strict experiment status check

Previous change was breaking reconnect by deleting lock files
when experiment status was not 'running'. However:
- Status field is never updated to 'completed'
- Scans complete but lock files remain (by design)
- This caused new experiment IDs to be created on resume

Simplified fix: If experiment.json exists, trust the lock file.
Only clean up locks if the entire experiment directory is missing.

Preserves reconnect functionality while still handling edge cases
where experiment directories are deleted.
